title: Pyrus bibas
taxonomy: Pyrus bibas is classified as a species of pear tree in the family Rosaceae, which is a large and economically important family of flowering plants. The family Rosaceae includes many other species of fruit trees and shrubs, such as apples, plums, and cherries. Pyrus bibas is a deciduous tree native to Asia, and its classification is closely related to other species within the genus Pyrus. The genus Pyrus is a group of pear trees that are known for their small, hard fruits and showy white flowers. The classification of Pyrus bibas reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species of pear trees.
uses: Pyrus bibas, also known as the Chinese pear, is a species of pear tree native to China. However, the information about its medicinal uses is limited and requires further research.
